name: Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: Adversaries may disable, modify, or bypass multi-factor authentication mechanisms to maintain access. In Entra ID / Microsoft 365 environments this manifests as MFA fatigue attacks (repeated push notifications to exhaust a user into approving), MFA method registration from adversary-controlled devices after initial account compromise, Conditional Access policy tampering to exclude attacker-controlled accounts, and SSPR (Self-Service Password Reset) abuse to downgrade authentication strength. Success means subsequent logins complete with AuthenticationRequirement 'singleFactorAuthentication' or with attacker-registered authenticators.
iocs: AuditLogs Operation 'Update user' with modified field 'StrongAuthenticationMethod' — MFA method added or removed
Burst of ResultType 50074 (strong auth required) followed by ResultType 0 within seconds — MFA fatigue approval
AuditLogs Operation 'Disable Strong Authentication' or changes to per-user MFA state to 'Disabled'
SigninLogs AuthenticationDetails showing MFA method 'PhoneAppNotification' with approval after >10 push attempts in 5 minutes
AuditLogs changes to Conditional Access policy excluding a specific user or IP range
New authenticator app registration (Operation 'Register security info') from an unfamiliar DeviceId or IPAddress
SSPR flow completion (Operation 'Reset password using mobile app notification') followed by MFA re-registration from new device
evasion_variants: Space MFA push notifications 90 seconds apart to avoid volume-based fatigue detection thresholds
Register the adversary's authenticator during a legitimate helpdesk-assisted MFA reset to blend with expected audit events
Exploit a Conditional Access exclusion group by adding the compromised account to that group rather than modifying the policy itself
Use number-matching bypass: socially engineer the user into approving the correct number displayed in the push — leaves no anomalous log field
Abuse SSPR with a pre-compromised backup email or phone to reset credentials and re-enroll MFA outside business-hours monitoring windows
Modify Named Location policy to include attacker IP so SigninLogs show ConditionalAccessStatus 'success' rather than 'notApplied'
detection_kql_hints: AuditLogs | where OperationName in ('Update user','Disable Strong Authentication','Delete user authentication method') | where TargetResources has 'StrongAuthenticationMethod'
SigninLogs | where ResultType == 50074 | summarize PushCount=count() by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m) | where PushCount > 8
Correlate AuditLogs 'Register security info' with SigninLogs IPAddress — flag if registration IP != any prior successful login IP for that user
AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Update policy' and TargetResources has 'ConditionalAccess' | project InitiatedBy, TimeGenerated, TargetResources
Look for AuthenticationRequirement == 'singleFactorAuthentication' on accounts whose prior 30-day baseline shows 'multiFactorAuthentication'
Flag ResultType 0 following a sequence of ResultType 50074 within the same CorrelationId session — indicates fatigue approval
AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Add member to group' and TargetResources has_any (known CA exclusion group ObjectIds)
